title: Market Brief — Monday, June 1, 2026
summary: GPU availability is loosening fast across every class, with util-avail at 31% for A100, 19% for H100, 34% for H200, and 45% for B200, meaning only H100 approaches scarcity while B200 is nearing oversupply territory. H100 spot surged 28.4% day-over-day to $3.60/hr, a volatility spike inconsistent with 16pp utilization declines over 30 days, signaling short-covering or contract expirations rather than genuine demand.
paragraphs: GPU availability is loosening fast across every class, with util-avail at 31% for A100, 19% for H100, 34% for H200, and 45% for B200, meaning only H100 approaches scarcity while B200 is nearing oversupply territory. H100 spot surged 28.4% day-over-day to $3.60/hr, a volatility spike inconsistent with 16pp utilization declines over 30 days, signaling short-covering or contract expirations rather than genuine demand.

At TCO-adjusted costs (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $13.16/M tokens, meaning DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output is selling at a loss of approximately $12.71 per million tokens. DeepSeek: Burning Cash. OpenAI gpt-oss-120b at $0.18/M output: Burning Cash. Anthropic Haiku 4.5 at $5/M and Google Gemini 2.5 Flash at $2.50/M maintain viable margins on cheaper silicon: both Neutral. Only premium-tier models like o3 Pro at $80/M output grade Strong.

B200 at $6.40/hr spot with 45% util-avail and 69% of offerings sitting idle is overvalued compute facing an imminent price correction as utilization craters 34pp in 30 days. Undervalued trade: lock in A100 capacity at $1.08/hr median where 47% annualized volatility is lowest across all classes and inference margins turn positive at high-tier pricing.
bullets: 45% util-avail on B200 marks the loosest supply condition across all GPU classes.
81% H100 utilization dropped 16.4 percentage points in 30 days, the sharpest deceleration among premium GPUs.
$3.60/hr H100 spot jumped 28.4% day-over-day despite falling utilization, suggesting a pricing dislocation.
21.78 LLMflation index means frontier-equivalent output costs 78% less than GPT-4 launch pricing.
$0.18/M output from OpenAI gpt-oss-120b undercuts TCO-adjusted production cost by over 95%.
197.8% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the most unstable compute contract to hold.
